blocks.label: About
blocks.value: First day of the Gregorian calendar year. Public holiday in Mongolia.

view.name: Цагаан Сар (Tsagaan Sar)
view.title: Tsagaan Sar
view.subtitle: Lunar new year — late January or February (varies)

blocks.label: About
blocks.value: Mongolian Lunar New Year and the most important traditional holiday. Three public-holiday days centred on the first new moon after the winter solstice.

view.name: Олон улсын эмэгтэйчүүдийн өдөр (International Women's Day)
view.title: International Women's Day
view.subtitle: March 8

blocks.label: About
blocks.value: Public holiday in Mongolia honouring the social, economic, cultural and political achievements of women.

blocks.label: About
blocks.value: Mongolia's national children's day; widely celebrated with family events and gifts.

blocks.label: About
blocks.value: Mongolia's largest national holiday. Features the 'three manly games' — wrestling, horse racing, and archery — and commemorates the 1921 People's Revolution.

view.name: Чингис хааны төрсөн өдөр (Genghis Khan's Birthday)
view.title: Genghis Khan's Birthday
view.subtitle: First day of the first winter month (lunar; usually November)

blocks.label: About
blocks.value: National holiday honouring the founder of the Mongol Empire, observed on the first day of the first winter month in the lunar calendar.

blocks.label: About
blocks.value: Marks the 1911 declaration of independence from the Qing dynasty and the restoration of Mongolian sovereignty.
